linear_regression.activation_notes: Identity — EML-0. Linear networks are EML-0.

logistic_regression.activation_notes: sigma(x) = 1/(1+exp(-x)). Rational in exp(-x): exp is EML-1, reciprocal adds 1 → EML-2.

shallow_tanh_mlp.activation_notes: tanh(x) = 1 - 2/(exp(2x)+1). exp(2x) is EML-1; rational in exp is EML-2; full tanh is EML-3.

deep_relu_mlp.activation_notes: Not analytic at x=0. ReLU networks are piecewise-linear → EML-inf.

siren.activation_notes: SIREN activation. EML-3 (same as pure tone). Dense oscillations → richer basis.

relu_resnet.activation_notes: Not analytic at x=0. ReLU networks are piecewise-linear → EML-inf.

EML-1.insight: EML-1 hypothesis class with 20 nodes: VC-dim ~ 60. PAC learning needs ~1259 samples (ε=δ=0.05). Depth k enters quadratically: EML-3 needs 9× more samples than EML-1 (for same n_nodes). This formalizes why deep networks need more data.

EML-2.insight: EML-2 hypothesis class with 20 nodes: VC-dim ~ 240. PAC learning needs ~4854 samples (ε=δ=0.05). Depth k enters quadratically: EML-3 needs 9× more samples than EML-1 (for same n_nodes). This formalizes why deep networks need more data.

EML-3.insight: EML-3 hypothesis class with 20 nodes: VC-dim ~ 539. PAC learning needs ~10845 samples (ε=δ=0.05). Depth k enters quadratically: EML-3 needs 9× more samples than EML-1 (for same n_nodes). This formalizes why deep networks need more data.

EML-4.insight: EML-4 hypothesis class with 20 nodes: VC-dim ~ 959. PAC learning needs ~19233 samples (ε=δ=0.05). Depth k enters quadratically: EML-3 needs 9× more samples than EML-1 (for same n_nodes). This formalizes why deep networks need more data.

EML-5.insight: EML-5 hypothesis class with 20 nodes: VC-dim ~ 1498. PAC learning needs ~30018 samples (ε=δ=0.05). Depth k enters quadratically: EML-3 needs 9× more samples than EML-1 (for same n_nodes). This formalizes why deep networks need more data.

shallow_tanh_mlp.verdict: EML-3L for L layers: universal approximator (Cybenko 1989)

siren.verdict: EML-3L: Implicit Neural Representation with sin activation

transformer_attention.verdict: EML-4L: GELU is depth 4; attention is EML-1 (linear softmax weights)

gaussian_process.verdict: EML-3: RBF kernel exp(-||x-y||²/2σ²) is EML-3 (squared dist is EML-2, exp is EML-3)

relu_resnet.verdict: EML-inf: ReLU activations dominate; skip connections are EML-0

key_insights.depth_not_layers: Adding more layers adds ATOMS (breadth) not DEPTH. Network depth in EML = n_layers × activation_depth. A 100-layer ReLU net is EML-inf (from first layer); a 1-layer tanh net is EML-3.
key_insights.relu_is_eml_inf: ReLU = max(0,x) uses the absolute value (kink at 0). Same barrier as tent map and Chua circuit (Sessions 51, 52). Universal but EML-inf — the same tradeoff as piecewise chaos.
key_insights.smooth_universality: Tanh, sigmoid, sin, GELU are all EML-finite (depth 2-4). The Cybenko (1989) universal approximation theorem says ONE LAYER of tanh suffices. In EML terms: EML-3 (one layer) already dense by Weierstrass. Adding layers increases depth but not expressivity class.
key_insights.pac_depth_quadratic: PAC sample complexity grows as O(k²·n·log n) for EML-k hypothesis class. Deeper functions require quadratically more training data (in the EML depth sense). This is a concrete generalization bound.

findings.name: ReLU = EML-inf (same class as piecewise chaos)
findings.content: max(0,x) has a kink at 0 — not real-analytic. Same EML class as tent map and Chua circuit.

findings.name: Smooth activations: linear=EML-0, sigmoid=EML-2, tanh/sin=EML-3, GELU=EML-4
findings.content: Activation depth is the EML depth of the activation formula. All smooth activations are EML-finite.

findings.name: Network depth = n_layers × activation_depth
findings.content: Adding layers multiplies EML depth. Adding neurons adds atoms (breadth). Cybenko: 1-layer tanh (EML-3) already universal.

findings.name: PAC complexity grows as O(k²·n·log n)
findings.content: EML-k hypothesis class VC-dimension scales quadratically in k. Deeper functions need quadratically more training data.

findings.name: Depth = efficiency, not power
findings.content: EML-3 represents sin(x) exactly with 1 atom. EML-1 needs infinitely many. Depth measures compositional efficiency, not closure expressivity.

next_session.title: Statistical Mechanics — EML Complexity of Phase Transitions
priorities: Partition function Z = sum exp(-βE_i): EML-1 Dirichlet-like series
Free energy F = -kT*ln(Z): EML-2 (log of EML-1)
Order parameter: continuous → EML-finite, discrete/kink → EML-inf
Phase transitions: EML-inf at critical point (non-analyticity of free energy)
